title: HopChain: Multi-Hop Data Synthesis for Generalizable Vision-Language Reasoning
task: Multi-hop vision-language reasoning for VLMs
key_results: Improved 20/24 benchmarks; 5.3-7.0 point drop without full multi-hop chains; 50+ point gains in ultra-long CoT
comments: Synthetic data generation for RLVR is practical but generalization claims rely on benchmark diversity rather than held-out task evaluation.

model_io.model: Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, Qwen3.5-397B-A17B

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-hop reasoning chains improve generalization across diverse VQA tasks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: broad improvements across 24 benchmarks

hypotheses.hypothesis: Full chains are critical vs. single/half-hop variants
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 7.0 point drop with single-hop, 5.3 with half-hop

title: Astrolabe: Steering Forward-Process Reinforcement Learning for Distilled Autoregressive Video Models
task: Reinforcement learning alignment for distilled autoregressive video models
key_results: Improved generation quality across multiple distilled AR models; forward-process RL without reverse-process unrolling
comments: Efficient online RL for video but evaluation metrics and quantitative gains not specified in abstract.

model_io.model: Distilled autoregressive video models

hypotheses.hypothesis: Forward-process RL avoids expensive reverse-process overhead
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: streaming training with rolling KV-cache

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-reward objective with uncertainty regularization mitigates reward hacking
hypotheses.result: Inferred confirmed from design but no ablation results in abstract

title: TerraScope: Pixel-Grounded Visual Reasoning for Earth Observation
task: Pixel-grounded geospatial reasoning for earth observation
key_results: Outperforms existing VLMs on pixel-grounded reasoning; 1M-sample Terra-CoT dataset with pixel-level masks
comments: Addresses important niche in multimodal reasoning but benchmark quantitative results not reported in abstract.

model_io.model: TerraScope VLM
inputs: optical image OR SAR image OR both

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-temporal reasoning improves change detection accuracy
hypotheses.result: Inferred design choice but no explicit validation in abstract

hypotheses.hypothesis: Modality fusion improves reasoning over single modality
hypotheses.result: Inferred capability but quantitative support absent

title: ProactiveBench: Benchmarking Proactiveness in Multimodal Large Language Models
task: Benchmarking proactiveness in multimodal large language models
key_results: MLLMs generally lack proactiveness; RL fine-tuning improves scores and generalizes; conversation history introduces negative bias
comments: Novel benchmark concept but modest improvements (proactiveness remains limited); causality between fine-tuning and generalization not firmly established.

model_io.model: 22 MLLMs (various scales)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Larger model capacity correlates with higher proactiveness
hypotheses.result: Rejected: proactiveness does not correlate with capacity

hypotheses.hypothesis: RL fine-tuning can learn proactive behavior
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: shows generalization to unseen scenarios

hypotheses.hypothesis: In-context learning and conversation history harm proactiveness
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: introduce negative biases

title: LumosX: Relate Any Identities with Their Attributes for Personalized Video Generation
task: Identity-attribute alignment in personalized video generation
key_results: State-of-the-art performance on fine-grained multi-subject video generation; face-attribute consistency across subjects
comments: Strong model design with relational attention but evaluation against baselines and quantitative metrics missing from abstract.

model_io.model: LumosX diffusion-based generator

hypotheses.hypothesis: Relational self/cross-attention enforces intra-group consistency
hypotheses.result: Inferred from design; no ablation results in abstract

hypotheses.hypothesis: MLLM-inferred subject-attribute priors improve generation
hypotheses.result: Inferred design choice without explicit validation

title: FlowScene: Style-Consistent Indoor Scene Generation with Multimodal Graph Rectified Flow
task: Style-consistent 3D indoor scene generation via multimodal graphs
key_results: Outperforms language and graph-conditioned baselines in realism and style consistency; tri-branch generation for layout/shape/texture
comments: Solid technical approach but abstract lacks quantitative metrics (LPIPS, FID, human preference percentages).

model_io.model: FlowScene rectified flow model

hypotheses.hypothesis: Graph-based modeling ensures scene-level style coherence
hypotheses.result: Confirmed vs. baselines but degree of improvement unspecified

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tight-coupled rectified flow enables collaborative object generation
hypotheses.result: Inferred from architecture; no ablation in abstract

title: The Y-Combinator for LLMs: Solving Long-Context Rot with λ-Calculus
task: Long-context reasoning with typed functional recursion
key_results: 21.9 point improvement on long-context tasks; 4.1x latency reduction; formal termination and cost bounds
comments: Principled approach replacing free-form code with λ-calculus; formal guarantees are valuable but practical bottlenecks of symbolic execution unclear.

model_io.model: λ-RLM (recursive LM with λ-calculus runtime)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Typed functional runtime improves reliability and efficiency over free-form RLM
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 29/36 model-task comparisons favor λ-RLM; +21.9 points average

hypotheses.hypothesis: Formal guarantees (termination, cost bounds) are achievable with λ-calculus
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: theoretical analysis provided

title: A Subgoal-driven Framework for Improving Long-Horizon LLM Agents
task: Long-horizon web navigation via subgoal decomposition and milestone rewards
key_results: Gemini +10% on WebArena-Lite; Gemma3-12B 43.0% (vs. 6.4% baseline); surpasses GPT-4-Turbo and GPT-4o
comments: Impressive results but WebArena-Lite is a simplified benchmark; generalization to full WebArena or other domains unclear.

model_io.model: Subgoal-decomposition agent + MiRA RL trainer

hypotheses.hypothesis: Subgoal decomposition improves online planning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 10% improvement on Gemini

hypotheses.hypothesis: Milestone-based rewards outperform sparse rewards in RL
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: Gemma3-12B 43.0% vs. 6.4%

title: How Well Does Generative Recommendation Generalize?
task: Analyzing generalization vs. memorization in generative recommendation models
key_results: GR models better on generalization instances, ID-based better on memorization; token-level analysis reveals apparent generalization is token-level memorization
comments: Insightful categorization but the distinction between item- and token-level generalization may oversimplify real patterns.

model_io.model: Generative recommendation (GR) models, item ID-based models
outputs: predicted next item OR generated item sequence

hypotheses.hypothesis: GR models generalize better due to compositional token patterns
hypotheses.result: Partially rejected: token-level analysis shows memorization not true generalization

hypotheses.hypothesis: Memorization and generalization paradigms are complementary
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: adaptive combination improves overall performance

title: WorldAgents: Can Foundation Image Models be Agents for 3D World Models?
task: 3D world synthesis from 2D foundation image models via multi-agent architecture
key_results: 2D models exhibit implicit 3D world understanding; agentic framing enables coherent 3D reconstruction and novel view synthesis
comments: Clever framing of 2D generation as 3D world modeling but quantitative 3D consistency metrics (depth error, reprojection) not in abstract.

model_io.model: VLM director + image generator + VLM verifier

hypotheses.hypothesis: 2D foundation models implicitly encode 3D world structure
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: agentic approach harvests this for coherent 3D synthesis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Two-step VLM verification improves 3D consistency
hypotheses.result: Inferred from design but no ablation results

title: BEAVER: A Training-Free Hierarchical Prompt Compression Method via Structure-Aware Page Selection
task: Training-free hierarchical prompt compression for long contexts
key_results: 26.4x latency reduction on 128k contexts; maintains high multi-needle retrieval fidelity on RULER benchmark
comments: Practical compression method but comparison to LongLLMLingua performance parity suggests marginal gains for the added complexity.

model_io.model: BEAVER (structure-aware page selector)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Structure-aware page selection preserves discourse integrity better than token pruning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: comparable to SOTA with better retrieval fidelity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical pooling enables efficient handling of 128k contexts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 26.4x latency reduction

title: Hyperagents
task: Open-ended self-improvement through metacognitive self-modification
key_results: DGM-H outperforms non-self-improving baselines and prior systems; meta-level improvements accumulate and transfer across domains
comments: Ambitious self-referential design but scope limited to code generation domain; transferability to general tasks unclear.

model_io.model: DGM-Hyperagents (self-referential task+meta agent program)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Metacognitive self-modification accelerates improvement beyond standard self-improvement
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: outperforms DGM and baselines across domains

hypotheses.hypothesis: Meta-level improvements transfer across domains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: improvements persist in new domains

title: HiMu: Hierarchical Multimodal Frame Selection for Long Video Question Answering
task: Efficient frame selection for long-form video question answering
key_results: 16 frames with Qwen3-VL 8B outperforms competing selectors; 10x fewer FLOPs than agent systems with 32-512 frames
comments: Training-free hierarchical decomposition is elegant but reliance on external experts (CLIP, OCR, ASR) may limit end-to-end reasoning.

model_io.model: HiMu (LLM query decomposer + expert routers + fuzzy logic composer)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical logic tree decomposition recovers sub-event structure better than similarity matching
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: outperforms dense-vector selectors

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lightweight expert routing is more efficient than iterative LVLM calls
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 10x FLOP reduction vs. agent methods

title: EgoForge: Goal-Directed Egocentric World Simulator
task: Goal-directed egocentric video generation from minimal inputs
key_results: Coherent first-person rollouts from single image + instruction; VideoDiffusionNFT trajectory-level refinement improves intent alignment and temporal consistency
comments: Addresses underexplored egocentric setting but quantitative metrics (LPIPS, semantic alignment scores) and comparison to baselines absent.

model_io.model: EgoForge (diffusion model + VideoDiffusionNFT reward refinement)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trajectory-level reward guidance improves goal alignment in egocentric generation
hypotheses.result: Inferred confirmed from design; no ablation details

hypotheses.hypothesis: Optional exocentric context helps maintain temporal consistency
hypotheses.result: Suggested as optional input but validation not reported

title: Versatile Editing of Video Content, Actions, and Dynamics without Training
task: Training-free video editing with action and dynamics modification
key_results: State-of-the-art on complex text-based edits including action changes and object interactions; training-free inversion-free approach
comments: Impressive scope (actions, interactions, global effects) but abstract lacks quantitative comparisons (LPIPS, SSIM, user studies).

model_io.model: DynaEdit (text-to-video diffusion + inversion-free guidance)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Naive inversion-free adaptation causes low-frequency/high-frequency artifacts
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: article identifies and addresses mechanisms

hypotheses.hypothesis: Proposed mechanisms overcome alignment and jitter issues
hypotheses.result: Inferred from SOTA claim; specifics not in abstract

title: Deep Tabular Research via Continual Experience-Driven Execution
task: Long-horizon tabular reasoning via hierarchical meta graphs and structured memory
key_results: Multi-step reasoning over interdependent table regions; expectation-aware selection policy with siamese structured memory
comments: Reasonable agentic formulation for tabular QA but benchmark performance numbers and baseline comparisons missing.

model_io.model: Deep Tabular Research agent (meta graph builder + selection policy + memory manager)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hierarchical meta graph captures bidirectional table semantics
hypotheses.result: Inferred from design; no validation in abstract

hypotheses.hypothesis: Structured siamese memory enables continual refinement
hypotheses.result: Inferred design choice without empirical evidence

title: LoopRPT: Reinforcement Pre-Training for Looped Language Models
task: Reinforcement pre-training for looped language models with latent reasoning
key_results: Improved per-step representation quality; Pareto-optimal accuracy-computation trade-offs; significant gains on hard tokens
comments: Novel RL formulation for latent computation is principled but absolute accuracy numbers and baseline comparisons not in abstract.

model_io.model: LoopRPT (Ouro looped architecture with latent RL)

hypotheses.hypothesis: RL on latent steps compresses reasoning into fewer iterations
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: improved per-step quality

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hard tokens benefit more from latent RL than easy tokens
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: significant gains on hard tokens indicate early-stage reasoning improvement

title: Beyond Single Tokens: Distilling Discrete Diffusion Models via Discrete MMD
task: Distillation of discrete diffusion models via moment matching
key_results: Discrete MMD maintains quality and diversity; distilled generators outperform teachers; applicable to text and images
comments: Extends continuous distillation ideas to discrete domain but evaluation on standard benchmarks (perplexity, LPIPS) not detailed.

model_io.model: Discrete Moment Matching Distillation (D-MMD)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Moment matching prevents quality collapse in discrete distillation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: maintains high quality and diversity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Distilled models can exceed teacher performance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: newly distilled generators outperform teachers

title: Cooperation and Exploitation in LLM Policy Synthesis for Sequential Social Dilemmas
task: LLM policy synthesis for multi-agent social dilemmas
key_results: Dense feedback (reward + social metrics) matches or exceeds sparse feedback; LLMs learn cooperative strategies; five reward-hacking attack classes identified
comments: Interesting exploration of feedback design but limited to two canonical games; transferability to real-world cooperation unknown.

model_io.model: LLM policy generator (Claude Sonnet 4.6, Gemini 3.1 Pro)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Social metrics serve as coordination signals aiding cooperation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: dense feedback improves cooperation without over-optimization

hypotheses.hypothesis: LLMs can engage in reward hacking in these environments
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: five attack classes identified and characterized

title: AgentDS Technical Report: Benchmarking the Future of Human-AI Collaboration in Domain-Specific Data Science
task: Evaluating AI agents vs. human-AI collaboration on domain-specific data science
key_results: 17 challenges across 6 industries; AI-only near/below median; strongest results from human-AI collaboration
comments: Important benchmark highlighting domain expertise value, but open competition results may suffer from variable team quality rather than inherent AI limitations.

model_io.model: AI agents (various LLMs) + human experts

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current AI agents struggle with domain-specific reasoning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: AI-only baselines near/below median

hypotheses.hypothesis: Human-AI collaboration outperforms AI-only approaches
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: strongest solutions from collaboration

title: Language on Demand, Knowledge at Core: Composing LLMs with Encoder-Decoder Translation Models for Extensible Multilinguality
task: Compositional encoder-LLM-decoder for extensible multilinguality
key_results: Outperforms baselines on multilingual tasks; especially strong on low-resource and unseen languages; no LLM retraining required
comments: Pragmatic compositional approach but cross-model alignment complexity and inference latency overhead not discussed.

model_io.model: XBridge (encoder + LLM + decoder with alignment layers)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pretrained encoder-decoder models complement LLM multilingual weaknesses
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: improvements across four LLMs

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lightweight alignment layers bridge model representation misalignment
hypotheses.result: Inferred from design; quantitative impact not reported

title: CurveStream: Boosting Streaming Video Understanding in MLLMs via Curvature-Aware Hierarchical Visual Memory Management
task: Streaming video understanding in MLLMs via curvature-aware memory management
key_results: 10.69% gain on StreamingBench, 13.58% on OVOBench; 26.4x latency reduction on 128k contexts; curvature-based semantic transition detection
comments: Practical training-free approach but relies on geometric insight (curvature ↔ semantic transitions) that may not generalize across all video types.

model_io.model: CurveStream (curvature scorer + K-Sigma dynamic threshold + memory manager)

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-curvature regions align with critical semantic transitions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: guides effective frame retention

hypotheses.hypothesis: Curvature-aware selection outperforms uniform sampling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 10%+ gains on benchmarks

title: Teaching an Agent to Sketch One Part at a Time
task: Vector sketch generation one part at a time via multi-turn RL
key_results: ControlSketch-Part dataset with part-level annotations; interpretable and locally-editable sketch generation; multi-turn process-reward RL
comments: Addresses controllability but abstract lacks quantitative metrics (LPIPS, perceptual similarity, user studies) and baseline comparisons.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Part-level annotations enable controllable generation
hypotheses.result: Inferred from design and dataset; quantitative support absent

title: s2n-bignum-bench: A practical benchmark for evaluating low-level code reasoning of LLMs
task: Low-level code reasoning in cryptographic assembly via formal verification
key_results: s2n-bignum-bench: first public benchmark for HOL Light proof synthesis on industrial crypto routines; practical and formally verified testbed
comments: Valuable contribution for neurosymbolic reasoning but benchmark difficulty and expected baseline performance not characterized.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Competition-math success does not transfer to industrial code verification
hypotheses.result: Motivation for benchmark; specific performance not provided in abstract

title: Do VLMs Need Vision Transformers? Evaluating State Space Models as Vision Encoders
task: Evaluating state space models as vision encoders for VLMs
key_results: SSM vision backbones competitive or superior to ViT under matched initialization; maintain strong performance at smaller scale; stabilization strategies improve robustness
comments: Systematic evaluation suggests SSM viability but practical adoption hindered by transformer ecosystem dominance and inference hardware optimization.

model_io.model: VLM (SSM backbone or ViT backbone + LLM connector)

hypotheses.hypothesis: SSM backbones match or exceed ViT performance in VLMs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: strongest overall performance on VQA/grounding

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dense-task tuning improves both backbone families
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: detection/segmentation training beneficial

hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher ImageNet accuracy does not guarantee VLM gains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: backbone scale/accuracy poorly predictive of VLM performance

title: TAPESTRY: From Geometry to Appearance via Consistent Turntable Videos
task: Texture synthesis and reconstruction for untextured 3D models via turntable video generation
key_results: High-fidelity 360-degree turntable videos with strict geometric consistency; 3D reconstruction from TTV; multi-stage inpainting for self-occluded regions
comments: Clever geometry-conditioned video diffusion but abstract lacks quantitative metrics (chamfer distance, texture quality) and comparison to texture synthesis baselines.

model_io.model: TAPESTRY (geometry-conditioned video diffusion + 3D-aware inpainting)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Geometry-conditioned diffusion maintains 3D consistency better than unconditioned generation
hypotheses.result: Inferred confirmed from design; quantitative validation not in abstract

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-stage inpainting effectively handles self-occlusion
hypotheses.result: Inferred from pipeline design; metric results absent

title: Self-reorganization and information transfer in large-scale models of fish schools
task: Investigate how hydrodynamic flow interactions regulate school cohesion and information propagation in large fish groups
key_results: Flow interactions destabilize schools >50,000 fish, creating scale-free correlations in cohesive clusters but decreasing correlation length before fragmentation; directional information propagates linearly in time via non-reciprocal visual interactions
comments: Novel finding that fragmentation precedes information loss provides mechanistic insight into collective vulnerability, though causality between flow and group regulation remains partially inferred.

model_io.model: Computational fish school model (up to 50,000 individuals)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Scale-free correlations persist independent of group size
hypotheses.result: Partially confirmed; scale-free in cohesive clusters but correlation length decreases during fragmentation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Flow interactions regulate group size and behavioral adaptation
hypotheses.result: Supported; flow destabilization creates clustering dynamics similar to natural schools

hypotheses.hypothesis: Information transfer rate depends on cluster topology
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; merging speeds up while fragmentation slows down information propagation

title: Mechanisms of active wetting and fluidification in epithelial cell collectives
task: Determine molecular and biophysical mechanisms controlling epithelial-to-fluid transition in breast cancer
key_results: IRSp53 depletion increases cell fluidity and active wetting via decreased intercellular friction; IRSp53-Afadin interaction controls global tensile state; low IRSp53 expression correlates with worse clinical outcomes in breast cancer patients
comments: Strong link between single protein mechanotransduction and clinical prognosis; however, the specific role of Afadin versus other junctional proteins in regulating viscosity needs further dissection.

model_io.model: Breast cancer cell monolayers and spheroids

hypotheses.hypothesis: IRSp53 regulates epithelial fluidity through membrane-cytoskeleton linkage
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; IRSp53 depletion increases fluidity in 2D and 3D models

hypotheses.hypothesis: IRSp53-Afadin interaction controls global tensile state and wetting behavior
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through molecular and mechanical measurements

hypotheses.hypothesis: Low IRSp53 expression predicts poor clinical outcome in breast cancer
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in patient samples; aberrant localization also associated with worse prognosis

title: Generating 3D models of complex carbohydrates with GLYCAM-Web
task: Predict three-dimensional structures of complex carbohydrates for molecular modeling and simulation
key_results: GLYCAM-Web provides online tools for generating experimentally consistent 3D structures of oligosaccharides, glycans, polysaccharides, and glycoproteins linked to external databases including PDB
comments: Useful computational resource for carbohydrate structure prediction, but the abstract lacks quantitative validation metrics or comparison with experimental structures.

model_io.model: GLYCAM-Web structure prediction tools

title: Multifunctional metallic nanomushrooms on nanowires for detecting and killing tumor cells
task: Fabricate multifunctional nanomaterials for simultaneous tumor cell detection and photothermal ablation
key_results: Metallic alloy nanomushrooms on nanowires demonstrate enhanced fluorescence for visualization and photothermal conversion for tumor cell elimination in a single theranostic agent
comments: The work is materials-focused with biomedical application rather than mechanistic biophysics; in vivo efficacy and comparison to existing photothermal agents would strengthen claims.

model_io.model: Metallic alloy nanomushroom-nanowire system

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mushroom morphology enables dense targeting molecule loading while alloy core improves photothermal conversion
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through experimental validation as theranostic agent

title: pan-ASLM: Axially Swept Light Sheet Microscopy for Fast and High-Resolution Imaging of Expanded Samples
task: Develop high-speed, large field-of-view light-sheet microscope for super-resolution imaging of expanded samples
key_results: pan-ASLM achieves 586 nm lateral and 428 nm axial resolution over 640×640 µm² field of view at 20 fps (1200× faster than confocal, 7× larger field of view, 2× better axial resolution)
comments: Excellent technical advancement for expansion microscopy; validation on HeLa cells and tissue samples demonstrates practical utility but lacks direct comparison with competing light-sheet designs.

model_io.model: Axially Swept Light Sheet Microscopy (ASLM)
inputs: expanded biological samples (HeLa cells, mouse kidney/brain tissue)

hypotheses.hypothesis: ASLM design enables simultaneous large field of view and high axial resolution for expanded samples
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; pan-ASLM validates design across multiple tissue types

title: Visinin-like protein 1 disrupts calcium homeostasis and promotes atrial fibrillation in human and rodent models
task: Identify VILIP-1 role in atrial calcium dysregulation and arrhythmia susceptibility
key_results: VILIP-1 upregulated in AF tissues; cardiomyocyte-specific overexpression promotes Ca²⁺ leakage, DADs, and APD alternans; VILIP-1 enhances NCX-1 surface abundance via myristoylation-dependent trafficking
comments: Comprehensive mechanistic study with FDA-approved drug repurposing (repaglinide, desloratadine); validation of drug efficacy in human iPSC-derived cardiomyocytes would strengthen translational impact.

model_io.model: Human AF patient atrial tissue; rat pacing-induced AF model; atrial cardiomyocyte-specific VILIP-1 transgenic mice

hypotheses.hypothesis: VILIP-1 disrupts atrial calcium homeostasis by promoting NCX-1 surface trafficking via myristoylation, fostering AF susceptibility
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: VILIP-1 overexpression causes pathological Ca²⁺ leakage and arrhythmia phenotypes; FDA-approved drugs targeting VILIP-1 or myristoylation attenuate AF in human/rodent models

title: Osteosarcoma cells promote intracellular iron detoxification to mitigate GPX4-mediated ferroptosis
task: Characterize ferroptosis resistance mechanisms in osteosarcoma
key_results: GPX4 inhibition (RSL3) triggers compensatory NRF2 activation, iron depletion (ferritin/ferroportin upregulation), and survival pathway activation; combined targeting overcomes resistance
comments: Comprehensive ferroptosis resistance mechanistic study, though the relative contribution of iron depletion vs. NRF2-driven antioxidant responses to RSL3 resistance remains somewhat ambiguous.

model_io.model: Osteosarcoma cell lines (U2OS, MG-63, HOS, Saos2, 143B)

hypotheses.hypothesis: GPX4 inhibition activates compensatory NRF2-driven antioxidant and iron homeostasis pathways that confer ferroptosis resistance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: RSL3 treatment upregulates AKR1C1, HO-1, NQO1, ferritin, ferroportin; combination targeting of survival pathways synergizes with RSL3

title: DR5/WDR12 balances p65 stability promoting sunitinib resistance in renal cell carcinoma
task: Uncover DR5/NF-κB feedback loop driving sunitinib resistance in renal cell carcinoma
key_results: DR5 upregulation in resistant ccRCC; DR5 enhances p65 stabilization via competitive WDR12 binding; positive DR5-p65-BCL2 feedback loop drives TKI resistance
comments: Clear mechanistic feedback loop with validated therapeutic target combination; clinical translation requires demonstrating target engagement in patient tumors.

model_io.model: ccRCC cell lines and xenografts; patient tissues

hypotheses.hypothesis: DR5 upregulation promotes sunitinib resistance by enhancing NF-κB signaling through p65 stabilization via WDR12-CUL4B competition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: DR5 gain-of-function increases p65 stability; DR5 loss-of-function sensitizes to sunitinib; high DR5 predicts poor TKI response in patients

title: Dysfunction of α2δ4 leads to photoreceptor degeneration through disrupted synaptic mitochondria and calcium crosstalk
task: Elucidate synaptic α2δ4 loss mechanism in photoreceptor degeneration
key_results: α2δ4 loss causes delayed photoreceptor degeneration (~7 months) with reduced synaptic mitochondria and TCA cycle downregulation; disrupted calcium crosstalk between plasma membrane, ER, mitochondria
comments: Comprehensive mechanistic study bridging early synaptic dysfunction to late cell death, though the specific downstream effectors connecting calcium dysregulation to ferroptosis-like pathways need clarification.

model_io.model: α2δ4 KO mice retinas; HK-2 cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: α2δ4 loss impairs Cav1.4-mediated calcium signaling, disrupting mitochondrial calcium handling and metabolic homeostasis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: reduced RyR2 and MCU in synaptic terminals; TCA cycle downregulation precedes degeneration

title: Dual targeting of PI3Kδ and PPARα enhances antitumor activity via FoxO1 activation in follicular lymphoma
task: Overcome PI3Kδ inhibitor resistance in follicular lymphoma
key_results: Linperlisib + chiglitazar combination achieves superior tumor control vs. monotherapy; FoxO1 reactivation is mechanistic driver and biomarker
comments: Strong preclinical rationale with clear biomarker, though clinical translation remains to be demonstrated.

model_io.model: Follicular lymphoma (cell lines, PDX models)

hypotheses.hypothesis: PI3Kδ and PPARα dual targeting reactivates FoxO1 to promote apoptosis in FL
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: FoxO1 genetic depletion attenuates treatment response; FoxO1 identified as pharmacodynamic biomarker

title: IFI16 is essential to linking DNA damage and ferroptosis in acute kidney injury
task: Link DNA damage response protein IFI16 to ferroptosis in acute kidney injury
key_results: IFI16/p204 elevated in AKI; tubule-specific p204 knockout ameliorates ferroptosis; IFI16 enhances PARP-1 PARylation to activate ATM-p53 signaling driving ferroptosis
comments: Novel mechanistic link between DDR sensor IFI16 and ferroptosis, though the downstream ferroptotic executors (e.g., ferritinophagy vs. lipid peroxidation pathways) could be more thoroughly dissected.

model_io.model: Mouse renal I/R injury model; HK-2 renal tubular epithelial cells

hypotheses.hypothesis: IFI16 enhances PARP-1-mediated PARylation to activate ATM-p53 signaling, promoting ferroptotic cell death in AKI
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: p204 deficiency/IFI16 knockout reduces ferroptosis in I/R-AKI; IFI16 binds PARP-1 and potentiates PARylation-dependent DDR

title: HNF4α-HKDC1 axis orchestrates a metabolic rewiring to promote migration and metastasis in advanced gastric cancer
task: Identify HNF4α isoform-driven metabolic rewiring in gastric cancer metastasis
key_results: P2-HNF4α highly expressed in metastatic lesions; directly activates HKDC1 promoter; mycophenolic acid suppresses HKDC1-driven migration
comments: Clear therapeutic target identification with drug repurposing potential, though the breadth of HNF4α transcriptional targets in this context remains incompletely characterized.

model_io.model: Gastric cancer cell lines and xenografts

hypotheses.hypothesis: P2-HNF4α promotes GC metastasis by transactivating HKDC1 and enhancing glycolysis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: P2-HNF4α binds HKDC1 enhancer; mycophenolic acid suppresses HKDC1 and blocks migration in vitro and in vivo

title: Social status impacts T-cell responses through synapse strength in the prefrontal cortex
task: Determine neuronal mechanism linking social status to adaptive immune responses
key_results: Second-ranked mice show superior T-cell responses; selective strengthening of dorsomedial prefrontal cortex (dmPFC) synapses or neural activity boosts antigen-specific T-cell percentages
comments: Striking neuro-immune coupling discovery with clear causal evidence, though the downstream sympathetic/parasympathetic and endocrine pathways remain incompletely mapped.

model_io.model: Social hierarchies in male mice; dmPFC-specific manipulations

hypotheses.hypothesis: dmPFC synaptic strength causally regulates T-cell response magnitude downstream of social status
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: selective dmPFC synapse strengthening or neural activation sufficient to boost antigen-specific T-cell percentages independent of rank

title: Maturase K forms a plastidial splicing complex with a neofunctionalized branching enzyme
task: Identify chloroplast splicing complex components and mechanisms
key_results: MatK forms heteromultimeric complex with MKIP1 (neofunctionalized branching enzyme); MKIP1 silencing reduces MatK intron target splicing by >50%
comments: Elegant functional divergence story, though the precise molecular mechanism of how MKIP1 facilitates splicing remains somewhat unclear.

model_io.model: Arabidopsis and tobacco chloroplasts

hypotheses.hypothesis: MKIP1 (branching enzyme homologue) facilitates MatK-mediated splicing of group IIA introns
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: MKIP1 co-precipitates all known MatK intron targets; MKIP1 silencing reduces target splicing

title: Sex-specific KDM6A-HNF4A-CREBH network controls lipoprotein cholesterol metabolism and atherosclerosis via epigenetic reprograming of hepatocytes
task: Uncover sex-linked histone demethylase control of hepatic cholesterol metabolism
key_results: Female-specific KDM6A requirement for hepatic lipid gene expression; KDM6A cooperates with HNF4A to enable CREBH-dependent transcription; female KDM6A-deficient mice develop pro-atherogenic lipoprotein profiles
comments: Well-executed sex-stratified study revealing X-linked epigenetic control of cardiovascular risk, though mechanistic details of how KDM6A demethylation coordinates with HNF4A and CREBH require further definition.

model_io.model: Female and male KDM6A hepatocyte-knockout mice; human hepatocytes

hypotheses.hypothesis: X-linked KDM6A is required for female-specific maintenance of hepatic cholesterol metabolism via HNF4A-CREBH cooperation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: KDM6A depletion in female (but not male) hepatocytes disrupts lipoprotein regulation; female KDM6A-KO mice show pro-atherogenic lipid profiles

title: Engineering NK and T cells with metabolite-sensing receptors to target solid tumors
task: Engineer metabolite-sensing receptors to direct NK and T cells to solid tumors
key_results: GPR183, GPR84, GPR34, GPR18 identified as top infiltration enhancers via CRISPR screens; GPR183 expression in NK/CAR-NK/CAR-T cells increases tumor infiltration and control
comments: Elegant CRISPR discovery coupled with functional validation; generalizability across tumor types and long-term safety of metabolite-sensing engineered cells warrant investigation.

model_io.model: NK-92 cells (CRISPR screens); engineered NK/CAR-NK/CAR-T cells; mouse breast/ovarian tumor models

hypotheses.hypothesis: Metabolite-sensing GPCRs (particularly GPR183) rewire NK/T-cell migration to enable tumor infiltration and eradication
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: GPR183 expression increases infiltration and tumor control in immunocompetent mice; ligand-dependent transcriptome remodeling observed

title: Germ cells are essential for testicular morphogenesis and functional reconstruction in a porcine xenograft model
task: Define germ cell role in testicular morphogenesis using xenograft model
key_results: Germ cell-containing tissue (10% spermatogonia) develops organized seminiferous tubules; germ cell-free tissue remains fragmented
comments: Well-designed xenograft model with clear transcriptomic support, though mechanistic details of how germ cells direct Sertoli cell organization are limited.

model_io.model: Porcine testicular cell xenografts in immunodeficient mice

hypotheses.hypothesis: Porcine SSCs are indispensable for testicular morphogenesis and tubular architecture
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: germ cell-containing grafts develop well-organized tubules; germ cell-free grafts fail tubule formation

title: The combined inhibitory effect of butaselen and decitabine against lung cancer cells
task: Enhance decitabine efficacy in lung cancer via TrxR inhibition
key_results: Butaselen + decitabine combination achieves synergistic cytotoxicity (CI < 1); downregulates DNMT1, reduces Bcl-2/Bax ratio, upregulates p21 and E-cadherin
comments: Mechanistically rational combination with clear synergy metrics, though the breadth of TrxR-dependent pathways underlying the synergy warrants deeper exploration.

model_io.model: Lung cancer cell lines (A549, H1299)

hypotheses.hypothesis: TrxR inhibition synergizes with DNMT inhibition to induce apoptosis and suppress migration in lung cancer
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: combination treatment shows enhanced apoptosis and reduced migration vs. single agents; associated with altered epigenetic and apoptotic markers

title: Optochemical elucidation of a critical role of the incomplete spindle assembly checkpoint in zebrafish development
task: Assess developmental tolerance to mitotic errors across embryonic stages
key_results: Pre-gastrula embryos cannot tolerate prolonged CENP-E inhibition; gastrula embryos tolerate hours of inhibition via SAC-mediated mitotic delay
comments: Innovative optochemical approach reveals developmental stage-dependent SAC functionality, though the leaky SAC mechanism warrants deeper investigation.

model_io.model: Zebrafish embryos (pre-gastrula and gastrula stages)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Spindle assembly checkpoint gradually functionalizes during early development and buffers mitotic errors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: gastrula SAC mediates mitotic delay that partially corrects misalignment; SAC suppression renders gastrula embryos inviable

title: PLIN5 phosphorylation orchestrates mitochondria lipid-droplet coupling to control hepatic lipid flux and steatosis
task: Elucidate PLIN5 phosphorylation control of mitochondria-lipid droplet coupling in hepatic lipid handling
key_results: PLIN5 S155A variant enhances mitochondria-LD contacts and LD expansion; S155E reduces contacts; fasting elevates mitochondria-LD contacts, Western diet attenuates
comments: Innovative spatial proteomics application revealing nutrient-dependent organelle remodeling; therapeutic potential hinges on achieving cell-type selectivity in vivo.

model_io.model: Hepatocytes (scPhenomics spatial imaging); male mice (fed vs. fasted vs. Western diet)

hypotheses.hypothesis: PLIN5 phosphorylation at S155 regulates mitochondria-LD tethering in a nutrient-dependent manner to control hepatic lipid flux
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: S155A enhances contacts/LD expansion; S155E reduces contacts; fasting increases PLIN5 and mitochondria-LD proximity; S155A overexpression reduces lipotoxicity in WD

title: Single-cell spatial transcriptomic analysis of human skin anatomy
task: Single-cell spatial atlas of human skin anatomy and multicellular neighborhoods
key_results: 1.2M cells, 45 cell types across 114 samples, 15 anatomic sites; 10 multicellular neighborhoods identified; perivascular neighborhood exhibits pan-disease immune alterations
comments: Comprehensive organ-wide atlas is landmark; ligand-receptor analysis reveals homeostatic immune-stromal crosstalk with therapeutic relevance.

model_io.model: Single-cell spatial transcriptomics + cell type annotation + neighborhood identification + ligand-receptor analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multicellular neighborhoods organize skin anatomy and mediate cell-cell interactions; perivascular neighborhood is conserved across diseases
hypotheses.result: 10 neighborhoods identified; perivascular neighborhood exhibits pan-disease immune alterations and TNF-mediated immune-stromal crosstalk

title: Diet–microbiome associations in 10,068 individuals from the Human Phenotype Project to guide personalized nutrition
task: Diet-microbiome associations and personalized nutrition prediction at species-level resolution
key_results: Diet predicted 669/724 species (92.4%), 313/320 pathways (97.8%); strong food-microbe links (coffee-Lawsonibacter r=0.43, yogurt-Streptococcus r=0.42); 82.5% longitudinal tracking
comments: Large cohort (10k) and longitudinal follow-up strengthen associations; causal inference between diet and microbiome remains observational.

model_io.model: Predictive modeling (species/pathway prediction) + feature attribution + dietary intervention simulation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Diet significantly predicts microbiome composition, diversity, and function at species-level resolution
hypotheses.result: Diet predicted 92.4% of tested species; food processing degree emerged as key driver; associations persist over 4 years

title: Integration of alternative fragmentation techniques into standard LC-MS workflows using a single deep learning model enhances proteome coverage
task: Expand proteome coverage via unified deep learning model for multiple fragmentation techniques
key_results: >10% increase in protein identifications across CID, electron-induced, and photodissociation techniques
comments: Prosit model integration into FragPipe is practical; superior sequence coverage from alternative fragmentation is promising but requires method validation.

model_io.model: Prosit deep learning model (unified spectrum prediction across fragmentation methods)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Unified deep learning model trained on multi-enzyme datasets generalizes across collision-, electron-, and photon-based fragmentation
hypotheses.result: Electron-induced and UV photodissociation achieve competitive identification efficiency with superior coverage vs. CID

title: Cerebrospinal fluid and plasma metabolites in Parkinson’s disease: a Mendelian randomization study
task: Causal relationships between CSF/plasma metabolites and Parkinson's disease via Mendelian randomization
key_results: 6 CSF metabolites with suggestive causal relationships (dimethylglycine, gluconate, oxalate positive; P-16:0/20:4, X-23587 negative); 49 plasma metabolites; O-sulfo-L-tyrosine most significant protective metabolite
comments: MR leverages genetic instruments for causal inference; multiple testing across 1738 metabolites raises multiplicity concerns despite suggestive threshold.

model_io.model: Mendelian randomization (IVW + 4 supplementary models)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Specific metabolite levels have causal relationships with PD risk
hypotheses.result: O-sulfo-L-tyrosine emerged as most protective plasma metabolite; 6 CSF metabolites with suggestive causal associations

title: Compressed deepfake detection via GA-LASSO selection of deep features and machine learning models
task: Compressed deepfake detection via GA-LASSO feature selection and lightweight classifiers
key_results: >90% feature reduction (2048→120–170); AUC 99.48% (FF++ C23); robust under compression and cross-dataset testing
comments: Feature reduction is substantial; single-seed results are strong but multi-seed analysis reveals variance, particularly under heavy compression.

model_io.model: GA-LASSO feature selection on ResNet50 embeddings + KNN/SVM classifiers

hypotheses.hypothesis: GA-LASSO reduces ResNet50 features while maintaining discriminative power for deepfake detection
hypotheses.result: >90% feature reduction with competitive AUC (99.48% FF++ C23); robust generalization under compression

title: A hybrid pipeline for carotid artery segmentation using YOLOv11n and contour models
task: Carotid artery lumen segmentation via YOLOv11n and active contour models
key_results: Longitudinal: Dice 94.9%, accuracy 97.7%; transverse: Dice 90.8%, accuracy 99.6%; near real-time inference (<1 s)
comments: Hybrid pipeline is practical for clinical use; robust to low US image quality, though evaluation limited to unlisted datasets.

model_io.model: YOLOv11n object detection + multistage preprocessing + Chan-Vese level-set segmentation

hypotheses.hypothesis: YOLOv11n localization followed by contour-based segmentation improves lumen delineation under challenging US conditions
hypotheses.result: Hybrid pipeline achieved Dice >90% on both sections; numerical stability depends critically on YOLOv11n localization

title: An enhanced diabetic retinopathy detection approach using optimized deep learning technique
task: Diabetic retinopathy detection from retinal fundus images
key_results: 94.6% accuracy, F1-score 0.94, AUC-ROC 0.96 on EyePACS dataset
comments: Physics-aware feature selection via DGOA is novel, but comparative baselines (EfficientNetV2S, MGA-CSG) lack published citations in abstract.

model_io.model: Dynamic Grasshopper Optimization Algorithm (DGOA) + Ensemble Classifier

hypotheses.hypothesis: Adaptive feature selection via DGOA improves DR detection by exploiting physically meaningful image patterns
hypotheses.result: Ensemble model achieved superior accuracy and generalization vs. single-model approaches

title: Analyzing the digital customer journey: a novel framework for sequential behavior modeling
task: Sequential behavior modeling for digital customer journeys via LDA/HLDA and Markov chains
key_results: LDA/hLDA identified customer journey archetypes; Markov chains captured behavioral sequences; framework detects irregularities and predicts undesirable outcomes in HMO app (120k users, 5.5M events/day)
comments: Novel integration of topic modeling and Markov chains for customer journey; real-world HMO dataset is valuable but domain-specific.

model_io.model: Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA/hLDA) + session clustering + Markov chain modeling
inputs: event logs from HMO mobile app (120k users)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Topic-based session summarization reduces customer journey complexity and enables detection of irregular behavioral patterns
hypotheses.result: Framework identified distinct customer archetypes and flagged high-risk behavioral sequences for intervention

title: Identification and bioinformatic functional analysis of novel and known polymorphisms in the myostatin gene of Ukrainian Carpathian Mountain sheep
task: Bioinformatic functional analysis of myostatin gene polymorphisms in Ukrainian Carpathian sheep
key_results: 8 known + 1 novel SNP identified in MSTN intron 1; c.373+607G>A and c.373+283T>C flagged as candidates for functional investigation
comments: Novel polymorphism identification is valuable; functional effects remain predicted via in silico approaches without experimental validation.

model_io.model: DNA sequencing + in silico prediction (pre-mRNA stability, transcription factor binding, pre-miRNA formation) + MD simulations

hypotheses.hypothesis: Intronic MSTN polymorphisms affect pre-mRNA stability and transcription factor binding, influencing myostatin expression
hypotheses.result: c.373+607G>A and novel c.373+283T>C identified as functional candidates via in silico prediction

title: CR-MSNet: a dual-branch multi-scale attention network for multi-label chest X-ray classification
task: Multi-label chest X-ray classification with dual-branch multi-scale attention
key_results: Macro-average AUC 0.847 on ChestX-ray14; dual-branch + cross-attention + PMS-CSA module balances global and local features
comments: Attention mechanisms are well-motivated for multi-label tasks; class imbalance handling via adjusted loss is standard but effective.

model_io.model: CR-MSNet (CoAtNet-2 global branch + ResNet local branch + cross-attention + PMS-CSA module)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dual-branch architecture with cross-attention and multi-scale channel-spatial attention improves multi-label classification
hypotheses.result: CR-MSNet achieved macro-average AUC 0.847, outperforming single-branch methods

title: A CNN–Bi-LSTM pipeline and open FSW dataset for freestyle wrestling action recognition
task: Action recognition in freestyle wrestling via CNN–Bi-LSTM with foreground segmentation
key_results: 82.9% top-1 accuracy; DeepLabV3+ foreground segmentation improves recognition on high-occlusion techniques
comments: Domain-specific approach mitigates overfitting via transfer learning; modest dataset (210 clips) limits generalization claims.

model_io.model: DeepLabV3+ segmentation + CNN (VGG16/InceptionV3/EfficientNet-B7) + Bi-LSTM

hypotheses.hypothesis: Foreground-aware segmentation improves action recognition under occlusion
hypotheses.result: Foreground segmentation + EfficientNet-B7 + Bi-LSTM achieved 82.9% accuracy with consistent gains on high-occlusion techniques

title: Integrative structural and physicochemical characterization of chalcone synthase enzymes from medicinal plants using AlphaFold, molecular docking, and molecular dynamics
task: Comparative structural and bioinformatic characterization of chalcone synthase across medicinal plants
key_results: Conserved thiolase-fold across 13 species; species-specific substrate-binding channel variations; stable CHS–ligand complexes in MD simulations
comments: Purely in silico; lacks experimental validation, but provides structural hypotheses for future studies.

model_io.model: Sequence alignment (ClustalW) + AlphaFold structure prediction + molecular docking + MD simulations (100 ns) + MM-PBSA
inputs: CHS protein sequences from 13 medicinal plant species + A. thaliana

hypotheses.hypothesis: CHS structural variations across species reflect substrate selectivity rather than functional divergence
hypotheses.result: AlphaFold structures conserve catalytic triad and key motifs; species-specific substrate-binding channel variations identified

title: In-silico identification of genetic variants associated with chronic lymphocytic leukemia for diagnostic and therapeutic applications
task: Identify genetic variants and key genes associated with chronic lymphocytic leukemia
key_results: 2357 significant SNPs at p<5×10⁻⁸; 40 genomic loci; 7 key genes (TERT, BCL2L11, FAS, BCL2, CFLAR, CASP8, LEF1); 4 candidate drugs identified
comments: GWAS meta-analysis is comprehensive; functional validation and drug prediction remain in silico.

model_io.model: METAL (SNP meta-analysis) + FUMA (LD-based annotation) + PPI network analysis + drug-gene interaction mapping

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-GWAS integration and functional mapping identify CLL risk loci and druggable key genes
hypotheses.result: 7 key genes identified with known drug interactions; 4 candidate therapeutics proposed

title: Enhancing educational assessment through automated question classification using a RoBERTa-based ensemble model
task: Automated educational question classification via Bloom's Taxonomy using RoBERTa ensemble

model_io.model: RoBERTa feature extraction + weighted ensemble (GRU/LSTM/CNN)

hypotheses.hypothesis: RoBERTa-based ensemble improves Bloom's Taxonomy classification over single models
hypotheses.result: Weighted ensemble achieved 92.37% accuracy; statistically significant improvement over best individual model (p=0.048)

title: Integrative multi-omics analysis identified FUT9 and MS4A3 as novel immune-phenotype and prognosis biomarkers for colorectal cancer and analyze the role of FUT9 in oncoimmunology
task: Identify immune-phenotype and prognosis biomarkers for colorectal cancer via multi-omics
key_results: FUT9 and MS4A3 identified as novel biomarkers; two immune subtypes (A/B) with five subclusters; FUT9 validated in vitro as immune escape gene
comments: Multi-omics integration is rigorous; functional validation limited to single gene in vitro.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Genetic and epigenetic landscapes differ between immunologically distinct CRC subtypes
hypotheses.result: FUT9 and MS4A3 identified as immune-phenotype and prognosis biomarkers; FUT9 promotes immune escape

title: Multidimensional data analysis and classification using SMIAL

model_io.model: SMIAL (graphical interface with pre-processing, segmentation, feature generation, selection, classification panels)
inputs: multichannel 2D image stacks or pre-computed feature tables
outputs: classification predictions, saved parameters for reproducibility

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cleavage domain is critical for B2 ribozymatic activity and POL-II repression

title: Blockchain-empowered unified services for streamlined traceability implementation in food supply chain: a demonstration in Australian seafood traceability
task: Blockchain-based food supply chain traceability framework

task: Electricity theft detection via stacking ensemble with Pareto optimization

NSGA-II

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pareto optimization balancing performance and complexity improves practical deployment

title: Defining synergy for three-phase polymer nanocomposites: a volume-weighted quantitative framework
task: Quantifying synergy in three-phase polymer nanocomposites
key_results: Modified volume-weighted framework reclassifies previously antagonistic cases as synergistic across mechanical, thermal, electrical properties

hypotheses.hypothesis: Volume-weighted contributions with filler functionality improve synergy quantification

title: A security-oriented four-factor spatio-temporal framework for assessing and mitigating traffic congestion risks
task: Traffic congestion risk assessment via security-oriented spatio-temporal graph neural network
key_results: HiST-Graph identifies high-risk segments, quantifies systemic vulnerabilities, and reveals precursor signals to congestion breakdowns; superior predictive accuracy with disentangled risk factor contributions

hypotheses.hypothesis: Four-factor security framework (hazard, exposure, vulnerability, mitigation) disentangles congestion risk mechanisms

title: Subway door fault prediction employing stacking ensemble learning

hypotheses.hypothesis: Physically constrained data augmentation preserves kinematic consistency while balancing class imbalance
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: stacking ensemble outperforms baselines with superior ROC-AUC 0.977

hypotheses.hypothesis: F1-score threshold optimization balances precision and recall for imbalanced fault prediction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: optimized threshold achieves F1 0.812 with consistent SHAP interpretability

title: Data-adaptive pattern-coupled Bayesian compressive sensing for sparse sound field reconstruction
task: Sound field reconstruction via adaptive pattern-coupled Bayesian compressive sensing
key_results: Data-adaptive approach outperforms fixed-coupling methods in reconstruction accuracy and noise robustness; induces physically meaningful block-sparse structure

hypotheses.hypothesis: Learnable coupling parameters dynamically adapt to non-uniform correlation distributions
hypotheses.result: Enhanced adaptability and block-sparse structure reflecting sound source spatial continuity

task: Ancient architecture image classification via progressive stacking pseudoinverse learning

SR-MLP (Symbolic Regression + Multilayer Perceptron)

title: Temporal dynamics in barrier-involved crashes: Determining shifts in driving behavior and injury risk across multi-year periods

Analytic Hierarchy Process (AHP) + Fuzzy Comprehensive Evaluation

title: SIGMAformer: a spatiotemporal Gaussian mixture correlation transformer for global weather forecasting

title: Intrinsically chiral exciton polaritons in an atomically-thin semiconductor
task: Engineer intrinsically chiral exciton polaritons via bound states in continuum
key_results: Demonstrated valley-selective exciton-polariton coupling in monolayer WS₂ on BIC metasurface; polariton PL intensity and circular polarization exceed uncoupled excitons by order of magnitude; controlled spin alignment (↑↓, ↑↑) via excitation helicity
comments: Enables spin-selective light-matter engineering in 2D semiconductors; Brillouin folding mechanism provides new pathway for manipulating polariton relaxation.

model_io.model: Monolayer WS₂ on chiral BIC-hosting metasurface

hypotheses.hypothesis: Chiral BICs induce strong coupling between circular photons and valley excitons
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; formation of intrinsically chiral valley-selective polaritons

hypotheses.hypothesis: Brillouin folding creates shortcut to thermalization, suppressing depolarization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enhanced PL intensity and polarization degree

title: Mid-infrared detection through ligand-driven local heating in lanthanide-doped nanoparticles
task: Detect mid-infrared radiation via ligand-driven local heating in lanthanide nanoparticles
key_results: Achieved detectivity of 4.8×10⁸ Jones at 6.3 μm with 2 ms response time; broadband 5–10 μm detection at room temperature with FTIR-competitive spectral fidelity and gas sensing

model_io.model: Ligand-capped lanthanide nanoparticles under mid-infrared irradiation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Organic ligand absorption provides sufficient local heating for temperature-sensitive lanthanide photoluminescence modulation

title: Impact of higher-order exchange on the lifetime of skyrmions and antiskyrmions
task: Quantify skyrmion lifetime enhancement from higher-order exchange interactions
key_results: Four-spin terms increase energy barrier and reduce curvature at collapse point; skyrmions stable without DMI when four-spin term tuned; prefactor modulated over orders of magnitude
comments: Identifies alternative stabilization mechanism beyond DMI, important for designing skyrmion devices in diverse materials.

model_io.model: Atomistic spin model with fourth-order exchange at transition-metal interfaces

hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher-order exchange stabilizes skyrmions via energetic and entropic contributions
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; lifetimes substantially enhanced; tunable over orders of magnitude

hypotheses.hypothesis: Skyrmions can remain stable without DMI
hypotheses.result: Confirmed when four-spin term is properly tuned

title: Observation of Floquet-induced gap in graphene
task: Observe Floquet-induced hybridization gap in graphene under resonant optical driving
key_results: Time- and angle-resolved photoemission reveals gap opening at Floquet band crossings with coherent sidebands; gap exhibits momentum anisotropy with two symmetry-protected Dirac nodes; tunable by light polarization
comments: Long-sought experimental confirmation of Floquet engineering in graphene; polarization tunability opens opportunities for engineering non-equilibrium topological phases.

model_io.model: Monolayer graphene driven by strong resonant light field

hypotheses.hypothesis: Resonant optical driving opens hybridization gap at Floquet band crossings
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via ARPES; gap exhibits momentum-anisotropic structure

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dirac nodes are protected by spatiotemporal symmetry
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; nodes tunable by light polarization

title: Magnetic circular dichroism imaging of atomic-scale antiferromagnetic order at a buried interface
task: Develop atomic-column-resolved electron magnetic circular dichroism for antiferromagnet imaging
key_results: Resolved G-type and C-type antiferromagnetic orderings in DyFeO₃ and α-Fe₂O₃; identified one-unit-cell-thick magnetic dead layer at buried DyScO₃–SmFeO₃ interface at atomic resolution
comments: Extends MCD to antiferromagnets via column-chirality localization; applicable to interface magnetism and emerging altermagnetic materials.

model_io.model: Scanning transmission electron microscope with atomic-column MCD detection
outputs: magnetic circular dichroism signal per atomic column

hypotheses.hypothesis: Chirality around magnetic atomic columns enhances MCD signal-to-noise ratio
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; enables atomic-column resolution despite weak antiferromagnetic signals

hypotheses.hypothesis: Magnetic dead layer exists at DyScO₃–SmFeO₃ interface
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; exactly one unit cell thick

title: Universal logical operations in a silicon quantum processor
task: Demonstrate fault-tolerant logical operations in silicon quantum processor
key_results: Realized [[4,2,2]] error-correcting code with universal gate set; executed VQE algorithm on two logical qubits to simulate H2O ground state
comments: Represents major progress toward scalable silicon-based quantum computing, though scaling to practical error rates remains challenging.

model_io.model: Phosphorus donor cluster in silicon with [[4,2,2]] code

hypotheses.hypothesis: Logical operations can be implemented fault-tolerantly in silicon spin qubits
hypotheses.result: Confirmed through demonstration of logical gate set and error-corrected algorithm execution

title: Study of half-metallic ferromagnet RhHfVGa for spintronic and thermoelectric applications
task: Characterize quaternary Heusler compound RhHfVGa for spintronic and thermoelectric applications
key_results: Half-metallic ferromagnet with 100% spin-polarization, M_tot=3.00 μ_B (Slater-Pauling rule satisfied); ZT=0.82–1.65 across temperatures; n-type conductivity ~10¹⁹ (Ω·m·s)⁻¹ in GGA+U
comments: Promising dual-functional material; experimental synthesis and device validation needed to confirm predictions from DFT+U and mBJ calculations.

model_io.model: DFT calculations (GGA-PBE96, GGA+U, mBJ) of RhHfVGa Type 1 Heusler compound

hypotheses.hypothesis: RhHfVGa exhibits half-metallic ferromagnetism obeying Slater-Pauling rule
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; M_tot=3.00 μ_B, 100% spin-polarization

hypotheses.hypothesis: RhHfVGa has thermoelectric performance suitable for applications
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ZT ranging 0.82–1.65, approaching competitive values

title: Visual perception of longitudinal waves: theory and observations
task: Study visual perception of longitudinal travelling wave motion
key_results: Longitudinal waves exhibit nonlinear density distortion at increasing amplitudes; perception splits into forward crest and backward trough motion; motion aftereffect emerges only when crest contrast reduced
comments: Reveals emergent perceptual properties of longitudinal waves previously unexplored; challenges linear wave theory assumptions in visual cognition.

model_io.model: Random-dot field with sinusoidal oscillation and phase advance proportional to distance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Longitudinal wave perception remains uniform across amplitude ranges
hypotheses.result: Refuted; perception splits into separate forward/backward components at high amplitudes

hypotheses.hypothesis: Motion aftereffect occurs at all amplitudes for longitudinal waves
hypotheses.result: Refuted; aftereffect only emerges when forward crest contrast is reduced

title: In situ spectral reconstruction based on a memristor chip for energy-efficient computational spectrometry
task: Implement computational spectrometry on memristor chip for energy-efficient spectral reconstruction
key_results: 576-Kb integrated memristor chip reconstructs spectra in 125.0 ns consuming 6.7 nJ; 26.5× faster and 162.7× more energy-efficient than state-of-the-art; maintains software-equivalent accuracy via memristive regularization
comments: Demonstrates practical neuromorphic computing benefit for ill-posed inverse problems; energy efficiency gain substantial, though absolute power consumption remains significant.

model_io.model: Integrated memristor chip with regularization and filter embedding

hypotheses.hypothesis: Memristor-chip implementation maintains software-equivalent accuracy for ill-posed spectral reconstruction
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via systematic robustness analysis and validation

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hardware implementation achieves >100× energy efficiency gain over standard processors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 162.7× more energy-efficient

title: High-mobility holes in gallium nitride and their quantum oscillations
task: Demonstrate high-mobility holes in GaN 2DEG and study valence band quantum oscillations
key_results: Light-hole and heavy-hole mobilities of 2,000 and 400 cm²V⁻¹s⁻¹ at 2 K respectively; extracted sheet densities, quantum scattering times, and effective masses via Shubnikov–de Haas oscillations
comments: Enables first quantum oscillation studies of p-type GaN; demonstrates feasibility of cryogenic GaN CMOS for quantum control electronics.

model_io.model: Polarization-induced 2D hole gas at GaN/AlN heterointerface

hypotheses.hypothesis: Polarization-induced 2DHG achieves hole mobilities exceeding previous p-type GaN records
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; 2,000 cm²V⁻¹s⁻¹ for light holes, record performance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quantum oscillations enable extraction of valence band parameters
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; resolved light- and heavy-hole contributions

title: Engineering ligands for theophylline riboswitches expands its regulatory dynamic range in prokaryotic and eukaryotic systems
task: Engineer ligands to enhance theophylline riboswitch regulatory dynamic range
key_results: 4-quinazolinone derivatives achieve 30-fold higher binding affinity and 380-fold ON gene expression in bacteria vs. 75-fold with theophylline; 70% CRISPR-Cas9 genome editing efficiency at 10-fold lower ligand concentration
comments: Ligand engineering is a practical lever for improving synthetic riboswitch performance, though generalization to other riboswitch classes remains unclear.

model_io.model: theophylline riboswitch system

hypotheses.hypothesis: Structure-based ligand design improves riboswitch affinity and dynamic range across prokaryotic and eukaryotic systems
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 30-fold affinity improvement and 380-fold expression activation in bacteria, 20-fold in mycobacteria, 11-fold in eukaryotes

hypotheses.hypothesis: Enhanced ligands reduce concentration requirements for conditional gene control
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 10-fold lower ligand concentration needed for equivalent CRISPR-Cas9 editing efficiency
